panels.visual_description: Wide shot, Lush Watercolor style. The Scotia steamship in dry dock, showing the enormous triangular gash in its hull. Engineers are inspecting it. Muted colors, industrial feel.
panels.dialogue: Narrator: The accident to the Scotia was widely known. A hole two meters below the waterline... a perfect isosceles triangle.

panels.visual_description: Close-up, Lush Watercolor style. Professor Aronnax sitting in his office in Paris, reading a letter. Warm interior lighting, books in the background. He looks thoughtful.
panels.dialogue: Aronnax: The Minister of Marine invites me to join the expedition of the Abraham Lincoln.

panels.visual_description: Mid-shot, Lush Watercolor style. Aronnax and his servant Conseil packing trunks in their apartment. Conseil is calm and efficient, Aronnax is flustered. Sunlight streaming through a window.
panels.dialogue: Aronnax: Conseil! I am leaving for New York. We sail on the Abraham Lincoln to hunt the monster!

panels.visual_description: Close-up, Lush Watercolor style. Conseil looking calmly at Aronnax. Soft focus background.
Wide shot, Lush Watercolor style. The Abraham Lincoln leaving the Brooklyn pier. Crowds cheering, flags waving. The skyline of New York in the background. Bright and energetic.
panels.dialogue: Narrator: The frigate Abraham Lincoln was prepared to purge the seas of this formidable cetacean.

panels.visual_description: Mid-shot, Lush Watercolor style. On the deck of the Abraham Lincoln. Aronnax talking to Ned Land, the Canadian harpooner. Ned looks skeptical, leaning on the railing.
panels.dialogue: Ned Land: I have chased many whales, professor. But a unicorn of the sea made of iron? Impossible.

panels.visual_description: Close-up, Lush Watercolor style. Aronnax gesturing excitedly, trying to convince Ned. The sea is visible behind them.
panels.dialogue: Aronnax: But the science, Ned! The evidence supports a narwhal of colossal dimensions!

panels.visual_description: Mid-shot, Lush Watercolor style. Conseil organizing instruments on a table on deck, listening to the conversation. He is neutral and attentive.
panels.dialogue: Conseil: If monsieur says it is a narwhal, it is a narwhal.

panels.visual_description: Wide shot, Lush Watercolor style. Night time on the deck. The crew watching the dark sea. The moon is reflecting on the water. Tense atmosphere.
panels.dialogue: Narrator: For three months we patrolled the North Pacific. The crew was on edge.

panels.visual_description: Close-up, Lush Watercolor style. Ned Land peering into the darkness with a telescope. His expression is intense.
panels.dialogue: Ned Land: Look there! A strange light!

panels.visual_description: Mid-shot, Lush Watercolor style. Aronnax and Conseil rushing to the railing. In the distance, an intense electric light glows underwater. Blue and eerie lighting.
panels.dialogue: Aronnax: It's the creature! It's glowing!

panels.visual_description: Close-up, Lush Watercolor style. Conseil looking at the light, eyes wide. The light illuminates his face.
panels.dialogue: Conseil: It is moving towards us, monsieur! At great speed!

panels.visual_description: Wide shot, Lush Watercolor style. The creature ramming the Abraham Lincoln. Water spraying, the ship rocking violently. Chaos on deck.
panels.dialogue: Narrator: The collision was inevitable. A shock that threw us all off balance.

panels.visual_description: Mid-shot, Lush Watercolor style. Ned Land throwing his harpoon, but it bounces off the metallic hide of the creature with a clang. Sparks fly.
Mid-shot, Lush Watercolor style. Aronnax falling overboard into the dark, churning water. Reaching out. Panic.
Close-up, Lush Watercolor style. Conseil diving into the water after his master. Determined expression.
